description: Address 6 issues from code review: wire errors.py into production, fix NO_COLOR env mutation, cache is_available() in dashboard, remove redundant ImportError catch, fix format_box off-by-one, move exception handler imports to module level
steps: 1. Wire errors.py into the global exception handlers in api.py and dashboard/app.py using api_error_response()
2. Fix NO_COLOR env mutation: clean up env var after CLI completes (or ensure tests use CliRunner env isolation)
3. Move is_available() checks in dashboard/app.py to module-level flags evaluated once at import time
4. Remove redundant except ImportError in _auto_fetch_context after is_available() guard
5. Fix format_box off-by-one: clamp fill to max(0, ...)
6. Move exception handler imports (logging, datetime) to module level in both api.py and dashboard/app.py
acceptance_criteria: errors.py api_error_response() is used in at least one production exception handler
NO_COLOR env var does not leak between CLI invocations
is_available() is not called on every request in dashboard endpoints
_auto_fetch_context has no redundant ImportError catch after is_available() guard
format_box handles titles longer than all content lines without negative fill
Exception handlers do not import logging or datetime inside the handler body
findings: All 6 review issues addressed. errors.py is now used by both exception handlers (api.py and dashboard/app.py). NO_COLOR env is restored via ctx.call_on_close(). Dashboard uses _HAS_AUGUR_DATA module-level flag. format_box uses max(0, ...) for fill. All 251 tests pass unchanged.
